figures.caption: Figure 3.1. Schematic of the local two-dimensional Riemann problem under the tworarefaction-wave assumption. Under the present weakly compressible assumption, the interfacial state at x̂/t = 0 is identified with the star state located between the left- and right-running acoustic waves.

Figure 3.2. Schematic illustration of temporal quadrature points for the volume and face contributions in a one-dimensional local time-stepping procedure. The blue circles denote the quadrature points t em used for the volume contribution and the purely interior face contribution of element Ωe, while the red triangles denote the quadrature points t f,kg used for the common-flux contribution on the piecewise subintervals induced by the timestep mismatch across neighboring elements.
Table 4.1. Density errors and mass variation for the one-dimensional simple wave on nonuniform meshes.
Table 4.2. Velocity errors and mass variation for the two-dimensional weakly compressible shear wave on nonuniform meshes.
Figure 4.3. Mesh and instantaneous local time-step distribution for the Re = 100 cylinder-flow computation.
Table 4.4. Comparison of force statistics and Strouhal number for flow past a circular cylinder at Re = 100.
Figure 4.4. Time histories of the drag and lift coefficients for the Re = 100 cylinder flow.
Figure 4.5. Velocity-magnitude fields, in m s−1, at t = 3.0 s for the Re = 100 cylinder flow.
Figure 4.6. Pressure fields at t = 3.0 s for the Re = 100 cylinder flow.
Figure 4.7. Pressure sampling line through the cylinder centerline and the corresponding pressure comparison at t = 3.0 s.
Figure 4.8. Computational grid for the lid-driven cavity calculation; coordinate lengths are in meters.
Figure 4.9. Post-processed flow fields for the lid-driven cavity at Re = 1000 with c0 = 10 m s−1.
Figure 4.10. Centerline velocity profiles normalized by Ulid for the lid-driven cavity at Re = 1000.
Figure 4.11. Dimensionless centerline pressure profiles and local enlarged views for the lid-driven cavity at Re = 1000.
Table 4.5. Velocity extrema normalized by Ulid on the cavity centerlines at Re = 1000.
Figure 4.12. Instantaneous element time-step distributions, in seconds, for the liddriven cavity calculation.
Figure 4.14. Computational mesh of the 30P30N high-lift airfoil.
Figure 4.15. Instantaneous Q-criterion iso-surface (Q = 2×107) in the slat cove region, colored by velocity magnitude.
Figure 4.16. Time-averaged Cp distributions on the airfoil midplane.
Figure 4.17. Schematic of the wall surface pressure probes used in this study.
Figure 4.18. Wall pressure spectra compared with experimental datasets.
Figure 4.19. Instantaneous time step distribution for the 30P30N high-lift benchmark.
